q: The chemical compounds found in living things are referred to as ___ compounds.
Organic compounds hold the key to ___ on the Earth.
The majority of biochemical compounds are big organic molecules that the body uses to carry out numerous biochemical ___.
These enormous molecules are termed ___.
The simplest organic compounds that contain only the elements carbon and hydrogen are called ___.
Hydrocarbons include aliphatic hydrocarbons (alkanes, alkenes, alkynes) and ___ hydrocarbons.
___ can be used as solvents in marker pens, medicines, and cosmetics and as fuels.
Ethers can be used as organic solvents in the ___ and aroma industries.
The presence of simple ___ compounds is responsible for the pleasant odour of several flowers.
The characteristic odour of cherries is due to ___.
The main component of nail polish remover is ___.
Acetic acid is the common name for ___ acid.
Aspirin is a derivative of ___ acid.
___ are also found in many molecules essential to life, such as amino acids, hormones, and DNA.
An ether is an organic compound with an ___ atom bonded to two alkyl groups.
In a ___ ether, the two alkyl groups are the same.
Ethers have the general formula ___.
In the IUPAC system, the oxygen atom and the smaller alkyl group are named as an ___ substituent.
Ethers can be obtained from alcohol by the elimination of a molecule of ___ from two molecules of alcohol.
Methoxymethane and methoxyethane are ___ gases at room temperature.
There is no intermolecular ___ bonding between ether molecules.
On heating with concentrated hydrogen iodide ($HI$), the ___ bond in ethers breaks forming alcohol and alkyl iodide.
Ethoxyethane was used for many years as a surgical ___ agent.
___ are organic compounds which contain the carbonyl group, $>C=O$.
In aldehydes, a carbonyl group located at the end of a carbon chain is bonded to at least one ___ atom.
Aldehydes and ketones have the general formula ___ and form a homologous series.
The location of the substituent in the carbon chain is indicated by the Greek letters ___.
An ___ carbon is a carbon atom bonded to a functional group.
The systematic (IUPAC) names are derived from the names of the ___ hydrocarbons.
For aldehydes, the suffix ___ instead of $-e$ is added to the characteristic stem.
The common name of a ___ consists of the names of the groups attached to the carbonyl group followed by the term ketone.
The simplest ketone is called ___.
In the oxidation of alcohols method, ___ alcohols give aldehydes.
___ represents nascent oxygen (oxygen atom) produced from the oxidising agent.
The aldehyde product is obtained by ___ to remove it immediately from the reaction mixture.
To produce a ketone, ___ alcohol must be heated with the oxidising agent.
The addition of water to ___ in the presence of $H_{2}SO_{4}$ and $HgSO_{4}$ produces ethanal.
At room temperature, ___ is a gas and ethanal is a volatile liquid.
Aldehydes are easily oxidised to ___ acids with common oxidising agents.
Aldehydes and ketones are easily reduced to the corresponding primary and secondary alcohols, using the reducing agent ___.
Hydrogen cyanide, $HCN$, adds across the ___ double bond in aldehydes and some ketones.
Formalin is a ___ % aqueous solution of methanal.
___ aldehyde is used in some perfumes of natural, sweet, or fruity scents.
___ are organic compounds with a carboxyl functional group $-COOH$.
Carboxylic acids form a homologous series with the general formula ___.
Formic acid was obtained by distillation of ___.
___ acid occurs in vinegar.
Since a carboxylic acid group must always be found at the ___ of a carbon chain, it is always given the "1" location position in numbering.
Ethanoic acid is formed by the oxidation of ___.
Carboxylic acids which contain up to ___ carbon atoms are colourless liquids with an unpleasant smell.
Carboxylic acids are ___ acids, and they can react with reactive metals.
Carboxylic acids react with carbonates to give salt, water and ___.
Carboxylic acids are reduced to their corresponding ___ alcohols using the reducing agent $LiAlH_{4}$.
The popular painkiller, aspirin, is the ___ derivative of salicylic acid.
___ are carboxylic acid derivatives in which the hydroxyl ($-OH$) group has been replaced by an alkoxy ($-OR$) group.
Carboxylic acid can react with alcohol to form ester in a process which is known as ___.
Carboxylic acid esters of low molecular mass are colourless, volatile liquids with ___ odours.
The most common method of ___ is the reaction of the ester with an alcohol in the presence of an acid catalyst.
Many esters occur naturally and are responsible for the flavours in ___.
Naturally-occurring esters mixed with $NaOH$ produce ___ that is used for washing dishes and clothes.
An ___ is a compound in which one or more hydrogen atoms of ammonia have been replaced by alkyl groups.
Amines are classified as primary, secondary, and ___, depending on how many alkyl groups are attached to the nitrogen.
Liquid amines possess a ___ smell.
The ___ reaction is used to identify the primary amines.
___ are carboxylic acid derivatives in which the $-OH$ of the carboxylic acid has been replaced by $-NH_{2}, -NHR, \text{ or } -NR_{2}$ of an amine.
The addition of ammonia ($NH_{3}$) to a carboxylic acid forms an ___.
The formation of an amide from ammonia and a carboxylic acid is a ___ reaction.
___ is a liquid while all simple amides are solids.
Amides containing ___ or fewer carbon atoms are water soluble.
